questions.question: Describe the main components of a virtual machine with a suitable diagram.
keyPoints: Virtual Memory - RAM allocation and management
Virtual Storage - disk types and formats
Virtual Network - vNIC and virtual switches
questions.modelAnswer: A VM consists of: 1) vCPU - virtual processors scheduled on physical cores, 2) Virtual Memory - RAM allocated with techniques like ballooning and TPS, 3) Virtual Storage - disk files (VMDK, QCOW2, VHD) that can be thick or thin provisioned, 4) Virtual Network - vNICs connected to virtual switches. These components are managed by the hypervisor.

questions.question: Compare thick provisioning and thin provisioning for virtual disks. When would you use each?
keyPoints: Thin: Grows as data is written

questions.question: Explain live migration of virtual machines. What are its requirements and benefits?
keyPoints: Definition: Moving running VM without downtime
Requirements: Shared storage, compatible CPUs, network
Benefits: Maintenance, load balancing, disaster recovery

questions.question: What are VM snapshots? Discuss their use cases and best practices.
keyPoints: Best practices: short-term, limit depth, don't use as backup
